creator: asami@cs.berkeley.edu
text:
*  : Is that dependency normal for packages that use the GNU autoconf? 
 *  
 *  I think so.

I think you guys may not be talking about the same thing.  If you want 
to use GNU autoconf to regenerate the "configure" script from
"configure.in", you will need a BUILD_DEPENDS line to devel/autoconf.

On the other hand, if your port just uses a GNU autoconf-generated
"configure" script to generate "Makefile" from "Makefile.in", you
don't need any dependency.

There used to be only one port that depends on autoconf.  (There isn't 
any now, according to ports/INDEX; I don't remember which one it was.) -- @

 *  I'd patch the rule out of the Makefile, and make a patch such that you
 *  get the same results as if you had run the autoheader yourself.  That
 *  is, a patch to the files that autoheader generates.  Heck, if you did
 *  that in the right order, you wouldn't need to patch the Makefile at
 *  all, since things would be up to date, right?

Well, what is the best way to do it depends on how the diffs turn out.
IIRC, the @ port above needed an enormous diff (more than a thousand
lines, I think) to configure which was only a few lines of change in
configure.in, thus the autoconf dependency was justified.

Satoshi
